title: Google Analytics and Segment load unconditionally at page start — telemetry fires before consent
exploit: Every page load transmits user-identifiable data (IP, user-agent, browsing behaviour) to Google and Segment regardless of whether the user has consented; violates GDPR Art. 7 and ePrivacy Directive requirement for prior consent for analytics cookies
fix: Load GA and Segment scripts only after positive consent is confirmed (e.g., inside acceptConsent()); use a consent management platform (CMP) to gate script injection
evidence:
<script async src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=G-XXXXXXXXXX">
  ...
  analytics.load('SEGMENT_WRITE_KEY');
  analytics.page();  // fires at page load, before consent banner shown

title: Email address and full name sent to Segment in raw analytics payload — PII leaves boundary without pseudonymisation
exploit: User's email and real name are transmitted to Segment (and any downstream integrations they forward to) on every login; violates GDPR Art. 25 data-minimisation and exposes PII to a third-party processor without explicit lawful basis or DPA
fix: Hash the email before sending (e.g., SHA-256) or omit it entirely; use an opaque internal user ID only; review all analytics.identify/track calls for PII
evidence:
email: user.email,   // PII: email address
  name: user.fullName, // PII: full name

title: Email, full name, and auth token stored in localStorage with no expiry — accessible to any page script indefinitely
exploit: Third-party analytics scripts already loaded on the page can read localStorage; XSS exfiltrates email, name, and live auth token in a single call to localStorage.getItem; data persists until explicit clear with no session expiry
fix: Store only an opaque session ID in localStorage (or sessionStorage for tab-scoped lifetime); never store the raw token or PII; for the token, use an httpOnly cookie

title: Consent banner shown after analytics already loaded — rejection has no effect on active trackers
exploit: The reject path sets a localStorage flag but does not unload GA or Segment scripts; both remain active and continue collecting data for the entire page session; user believes they have opted out but have not
fix: Gate script loading on a prior consent decision; on reject, prevent scripts from loading at all; if already loaded, call the vendor's opt-out API (gtag('consent', 'update', {ad_storage:'denied'})); use a CMP
